2026-04-18T17:56:34.7988458Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7989045Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7989492Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7989926Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7990279Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7990732Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7990899Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7991097Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7991278Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7991519Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7991723Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7992018Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-18T17:56:34.7992299Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-18T18:00:29.2249103Z Filed issue **[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Missing Usage/Prerequisites section in http-client-csharp-mgmt README** and dispatched triage.
2026-04-18T18:00:29.2250425Z 
2026-04-18T18:00:30.4683659Z **Summary of gap found:** PR #58323 added `regen-docs` automation to `Generate.ps1` and auto-regenerated the `http-client-csharp-mgmt` README using `tspd doc`. However, `tspd` only regenerates emitter option documentation — it dropped the human-authored `## Usage` section (Prerequisites and Customization Guide link) that exists in the parallel data-plane README. Neither README also documents the new `regen-docs`/`regen-docs:only` npm scripts for developers who need to manually trigger doc regeneration.

2026-04-18T18:00:34.0169539Z   **Summary of gap found:** PR #58323 added `regen-docs` automation to `Generate.ps1` and auto-regenerated the `http-client-csharp-mgmt` README using `tspd doc`. However, `tspd` only regenerates emitter option documentation — it dropped the human-authored `## Usage` section (Prerequisites and Customization Guide link) that exists in the parallel data-plane README. Neither README also documents the new `regen-docs`/`regen-docs:only` npm scripts for developers who need to manually trigger doc regeneration.

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-18T18:00:34.2204218Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-18T18:00:20.457341124Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #58323 by @JoshLove-msft\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58323 added `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts to both `http-client-csharp` and `http-client-csharp-mgmt` packages, and integrated doc regeneration into `Generate.ps1`. As part of this, the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` was auto-regenerated using `tspd doc`. However, the `tspd` tool only regenerates emitter option documentation — it does not preserve or generate the human-authored `## Usage` section.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is missing the `## Usage` section (with `### Prerequisite` and `### Customizing Generated Code` sub-sections) that exists in the parallel `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`\n- The Prerequisites sub-section (Node.js and .NET SDK version requirements) is absent, leaving mgmt emitter users without setup guidance\n- There is no link to a Customization Guide for mgmt-specific generated code (comparable to the one in the data-plane README)\n- Neither README documents the new `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts — developers modifying emitter options manually need to know they can run `npm run regen-docs` to regenerate the README\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md`\n\n**Edit** — Insert the following `## Usage` section after the `## Install` section and before the `## Emitter usage` section:\n\n```markdown\n## Usage\n\n### Prerequisite\n\n- Install [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/download/) 20 or above. (Verify by running `node --version`)\n- Install [**.NET 10.0 SDK**](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/10.0) for your specific platform. (or a higher version within the 10.0.\\* band)\n\n### Customizing Generated Code\n\nFor detailed instructions on how to customize the generated C# code for Azure management libraries, see the [Customization Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md).\n\n### Regenerating Documentation\n\nThe emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run:\n\n```bash\nnpm run regen-docs\n```\n\nThis rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter).\n```\n\n#### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`\n\n**Edit** — Add a \"Regenerating Documentation\" sub-section under `## Usage`, after `### Customizing Generated Code`:\n\n```markdown\n### Regenerating Documentation\n\nThe emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run:\n\n```bash\nnpm run regen-docs\n```\n\nThis rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter).\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNot applicable — these are tool/generator package READMEs, not Azure SDK client library packages. No snippet test files apply.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` should contain these sections after the fix:\n1. `## Install` ✅ (already present)\n2. `## Usage` ❌ **missing** — add it\n   - `### Prerequisite`\n   - `### Customizing Generated Code`\n   - `### Regenerating Documentation`\n3. `## Emitter usage` ✅ (already present)\n4. `## Emitter options` ✅ (already present, auto-generated)\n\nReference: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` for section formatting and tone.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order from the repository root:\n\n1. Verify the README changes look correct manually — no build is needed for pure documentation edits to these generator packages\n2. Optionally, to confirm `regen-docs` still works after the edit (note: `tspd` only regenerates the `## Emitter options` section, so the `## Usage` section added manually will be preserved):\n   ```powershell\n   cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt\n   npm install\n   npm run regen-docs\n   ```\n   Check that the `## Usage` section is still present in the regenerated file (it should be, since `tspd` only affects the emitter options sections it generates).\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","temporary_id":"aw_docs58323","title":"[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Missing Usage/Prerequisites section in http-client-csharp-mgmt README"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully."
2026-04-18T18:00:34.2638202Z       },
2026-04-18T18:00:34.2638291Z       {
2026-04-18T18:00:34.2638386Z         "name": "dispatch_triage",
